2. ABSTRACT
This comprehensive report summarizes the intensive training programs in Generative Artificial Intelligence that
I completed across three premier platforms: Google Kaggle's 5-Day Gen AI Intensive Course (extended to 30
days), Outskill's 2-Day Gen AI Mastermind Course, and GUVI's Prompt Engineering Certification Course (2
weeks). The primary objective of these programs was to gain foundational-to-advanced knowledge of
generative AI technologies, large language models, transformer architectures, prompt engineering techniques,
and real-world AI application development.
3. INTRODUCTION
In today's digital era, Generative Artificial Intelligence  has emerged as one of the most revolutionary and

s digital era, Generative Artificial Intelligence  has emerged as one of the most revolutionary and
rapidly advancing fields in technology. Organizations across the world are leveraging generative AI to automate
content creation, enhance customer experiences, develop intelligent chatbots, generate code, create visual
content, and solve complex problems that were previously thought to require human creativity. Generative AI
combines elements of deep learning, natural language processing, computer vision, and advanced neural
network architectures to create new content and insights from patterns learned from vast amounts of data.
The field of Generative AI has witnessed unprecedented growth with the emergence of large language models

eld of Generative AI has witnessed unprecedented growth with the emergence of large language models
like GPT, Claude, Gemini, and LLaMA, image generation models like DALL-E and Stable Diffusion, and
multimodal AI systems that can process and generate multiple types of content. These technologies are
transforming industries including healthcare, finance, education, entertainment, software development, and
creative arts.
The purpose of these intensive training programs was to help me understand both fundamental and advanced
concepts in Generative AI and provide hands-on experience through practical tasks, coding exercises, and real-
world projects. Over the course of 45+ days across three platforms, I learned how to work with transformer

Processing (NLP) and deep learning methodologies. This project addresses the critical 
challenges f aced in manual legal document review, including high operational costs, 
significant time delays, and potential human errors. The system employs transformer -based 
models fine-tuned on legal corpora to generate both extractive and abstractive summaries 
with precise clause-level citations.

INTRODUCTION

1.1 OVERVIEW

The ability to interpret human emotions accurately has become a crucial aspect of modern artificial intelligence applications, particularly in human–computer interaction. Emotions play a key role in communication, decision-making, and mental health, and recognizing them through facial expressions provides valuable insight into a person’s psychological state. This project focuses on developing an RNN-based Facial Emotion Recognition and Feedback Generation System, which identifies emotions from facial images or videos and provides real-time feedback messages to the user. Unlike traditional systems that rely on manual observation or static image analysis, this project leverages the strength of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to analyze sequential facial data, enabling a deeper

e strength of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to analyze sequential facial data, enabling a deeper understanding of temporal emotional changes over time.

The system workflow begins with the input, which can be a single image or a continuous video. When a video input is received, it is decomposed into multiple frames, and each frame undergoes preprocessing steps such as face detection, cropping, and normalization to ensure consistent analysis. These processed frames are then passed through the RNN model, which captures sequential dependencies between frames to identify emotional states such as happiness, sadness, anger, disgust, or surprise.
ween frames to identify emotional states such as happiness, sadness, anger, disgust, or surprise.

Since emotions are not static but evolve over time, the RNN model is ideal for recognizing subtle changes in expressions, allowing for high accuracy even in dynamic facial movements or varying lighting conditions. Input Acquisition Module – Captures images or videos from the user or camera.

Emotion Recognition Module – Uses an RNN model to identify emotions from sequential frames, capturing temporal changes in expressions.
Feedback Generation Module – Maps each detected emotion to predefined feedback messages that motivate or comfort the user.

The system faces certain design constraints that influence its development and performance. The first constraint is data quality, as emotion recognition models require diverse and high-quality facial datasets for effective training. Lighting conditions, facial occlusions, and camera quality can also impact accuracy. Computational resources are another important constraint, since real-time video processing and deep learning inference need good processing power and memory. The system must be optimized to run efficiently on standard hardware. User privacy is a key concern, as facial data is sensitive and must be handled securely. Model interpretability is also important, ensuring that the system’s decisions can be understood and trusted by users. Additionally, real-time performance places

stem’s decisions can be understood and trusted by users. Additionally, real-time performance places constraints on processing time, requiring fast and lightweight model execution. Scalability and maintainability are also considered, so the system can be easily updated with new models or datasets in the future. By addressing these constraints, the design ensures that the system remains efficient, secure, and user-friendly.

7.4 SOFTWARE DETAILED DESIGN

The software design defines how each module interacts and how data flows within the system. The process starts when the user provides an image or video. For video input, frames are extracted and sent to the Preprocessing Module, where face detection and normalization take place. The preprocessed frames are then passed to the RNN model, which analyzes temporal patterns to classify the emotion. After classification, the Feedback Generation Module maps the detected emotion to an appropriate text message stored in a predefined list or database. The User Interface Module displays both the detected emotion and the generated message in real time. The system is implemented using Python with libraries like TensorFlow/PyTorch for deep learning and OpenCV for image processing. The design follows a

ies like TensorFlow/PyTorch for deep learning and OpenCV for image processing. The design follows a modular and scalable structure, making it easy to maintain and enhance. Real-time processing ensures fast responses, while the overall architecture provides accuracy, interpretability, and emotional relevance.

INPUT DESIGN

The input design specifies how data is collected, validated, and prepared for analysis. The system accepts two types of input:

Image Input: A single image that is preprocessed and analyzed for emotion detection.

Video Input:A video file or live camera feed, which is split into frames and analyzed sequentially.

ideo Input:A video file or live camera feed, which is split into frames and analyzed sequentially.

Each input undergoes face detection, cropping, resizing, and normalization to maintain uniformity. The preprocessing ensures that only relevant facial regions are analyzed and that variations in lighting or background are minimized. The design also includes input validation to ensure that the uploaded data is in the correct format and quality. This careful handling of input data improves the system’s reliability and emotion detection accuracy.

OUTPUT DESIGN

The output design focuses on presenting the recognized emotions and the generated feedback clearly and effectively. After processing the input, the system displays:

CONCLUSION

In conclusion, the Facial Emotion Recognition and Feedback Generation System using Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) successfully demonstrates how artificial intelligence can be leveraged to interpret human emotions from facial expressions with high accuracy. By processing both image and video inputs, the system effectively captures not just static expressions but also temporal emotional changes, enabling more reliable emotion detection. The use of RNN allows the model to analyze sequential patterns across frames, providing deeper insight into dynamic facial movements. The system’s capability to recognize emotions such as happiness, sadness, anger, fear, and surprise establishes its potential for real-world applications in areas like mental health support, online education, and

ts potential for real-world applications in areas like mental health support, online education, and human–computer interaction. Its modular design, including stages like feature extraction, emotion classification, and feedback generation, ensures flexibility, scalability, and adaptability to different environments.
eedback generation, ensures flexibility, scalability, and adaptability to different environments.

Beyond emotion detection, the project stands out for its feedback generation mechanism, which provides personalized and motivational messages to users based on their emotional state. This transforms the system from a simple recognition tool into an emotionally intelligent assistant that encourages positivity and emotional well-being. The project highlights the growing importance of emotionally aware technologies that can understand, respond to, and support human feelings in real time. With further advancements and integration into everyday applications, this system has the potential to make technology more compassionate, interactive, and supportive bridging the gap between artificial intelligence and human emotion.

Incorporating advanced deep learning models like Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) with attention mechanisms can further improve temporal understanding of emotional sequences.
